title: Cephalostigma erectum
intro: Cephalostigma erectum, also known as the 'Blue Lily', is a perennial plant native to Australia, belonging to the family Menispermaceae. The health benefits of Cephalostigma erectum include its ability to act as an antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic agent, making it useful in the treatment of fever, pain, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, including cephalostigmine and isoccephalostigmine, which have been shown to possess medicinal properties. Cephalostigma erectum is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Cephalostigma erectum is classified within the plant family Menispermaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as moonflowers.
